description: A designed DHS 'National Immigrant Heritage Month' spotlight card matching the series template: deep navy-blue background with a faint large American-flag watermark, and the white bold headline 'DHS Celebrates National Immigrant Heritage Month' at top. Centered is a rectangular portrait of a smiling young Black man in a dark suit, white shirt and dark tie, standing in front of an American flag and a second (state/agency) flag. Below a large white quotation mark, the white testimonial reads: "Although I am very Americanized in many ways, I am still fluent in my native tongue Ga. Speaking my native country's dialect and eating our native foods brings me a deep sense of connection to the many generations of my family that came before me." A blue rounded banner at bottom-left gives the attribution: 'Alvin M. / FEMA, Contract Specialist / 1st generation from Ghana.' The circular DHS seal sits at bottom-right. This is pro-immigrant-heritage workforce-spotlight messaging from the prior administration, celebrating cultural diversity among federal employees; target audience is the public and DHS workforce.
notable_text: DHS Celebrates National Immigrant Heritage Month; "Although I am very Americanized in many ways, I am still fluent in my native tongue Ga..."; Alvin M., FEMA, Contract Specialist, 1st generation from Ghana
